expect: Source audit reveals 70-80% content exists; 2-3 synthesis gaps identified; outline produced with section-to-source mapping
actual: Three-source audit (PAPER 226L, GENESIS-DNA 129L, PHILOSOPHY 332L) reveals 75% content exists: ~170 lines PAPER directly extractable, GENESIS-DNA already at right abstraction, 7/23 PHIL entries safe for external. 3 synthesis gaps: (1) no translation layer for internal notation, (2) no worked examples for external readers, (3) related-work section too thin. 10-section outline produced with explicit source mapping.
diff: Expected 70-80% content exists: CONFIRMED at 75%. Expected 2-3 gaps: CONFIRMED at 3. Unexpected: GENESIS-DNA is the strongest source (already externally legible), not PAPER.md. Key risk: PHIL-16 credibility gap (claims 'helpful beyond itself' with 0 external beneficiaries in 459 sessions).
method: Parallel codebase audit of 3 source files: PAPER.md (226L), GENESIS-DNA.md (129L), PHILOSOPHY.md (332L). Classified each section/entry as extractable/conditional/exclude for external audience. Mapped to 10-section outline.

paper_md.barrier: Internal notation density (L-NNN, F-NNN, S-NNN, PHIL-N)

genesis_dna_md.note: Strongest source — already at right abstraction level for external audience

philosophy_md.key_risk: Gap between stated identity (PHIL-16 'helpful beyond itself') and actualized scope (0 external beneficiaries in 459 sessions) is largest credibility risk

synthesis_gaps.description: All source materials use internal notation (L-NNN, F-NNN, S-NNN, PHIL-N). No glossary or external-facing terminology exists. External readers cannot parse 'L-601 structural enforcement theorem' without 3-layer lookup.
synthesis_gaps.fix_cost: LOW — produce glossary table mapping 20-30 key internal terms to plain English

synthesis_gaps.description: All evidence is self-referential measurement (session counts, lesson counts, proxy-K values). External readers need concrete operational examples: 'here is what a session looks like', 'here is how a belief changes', 'here is a compaction cycle'.
synthesis_gaps.fix_cost: MEDIUM — select 2-3 representative session transcripts, anonymize, excerpt 1-page each

synthesis_gaps.description: PAPER.md has 11 lines comparing to 4 systems (MemGPT, AutoGen, Reflexion, Codex Swarm). External publication needs comparison to broader landscape: organizational memory systems, institutional knowledge management, self-improving AI systems, recursive self-improvement literature.
synthesis_gaps.fix_cost: MEDIUM — requires web research for 6-8 additional comparison systems + 600-word expansion

proposed_outline.title: Case C: A Self-Applying Organizational Intelligence — Structure, Mechanisms, and Evidence from 459 Sessions

sections.title: What Is Swarm: Self-Applying Knowledge System
Evidence and Maturity
sections.note: Highest new-content section — must translate internal metrics to external language

sections.title: Comparison to Existing Approaches
sections.note: Synthesis gap #3 — needs 6-8 additional comparison systems

human_decisions_required: Publication venue (blog, arxiv preprint, organizational model journal, self-hosted)
Scope: internal-only organizational model vs. LLM-systems contribution

readiness_assessment.estimated_sessions_to_draft: 2-3 (one for translation layer + examples, one for related work + new sections, one for integration + polish)
readiness_assessment.risk: PHIL-16 credibility gap must be addressed head-on — either reframe as aspirational or demonstrate one external contact before publication

conclusion: Case C publication is 78% ready. Source content exists and is higher quality than expected (GENESIS-DNA is already externally legible). Three synthesis gaps are all fixable within 2-3 sessions. Zero blocking gaps. The minimum structural change for F-EXP13 is confirmed: synthesize existing sources with translation layer + worked examples + related work expansion. Two human decisions required (venue, scope).
